name: outlaw-platoon
description: Sean Parnell's visceral account of leading a platoon of American infantrymen on the Afghan-Pakistani border in 2006 — sixteen months of continuous combat, brotherhood forged in fire, and the emotional cost of war on the men who fight it.
useCase: Understanding infantry combat in Afghanistan, small-unit leadership, the brotherhood of soldiers, the psychological toll of war, and what it means to lead men in extreme conditions.
knownRisks: Extremely graphic combat descriptions and death scenes
The author admits to changing names of some soldiers for privacy
Written from a single participant's perspective — not a strategic overview
Contains honest depictions of soldiers' flaws and failures, not just heroism
